question_groups.instruction:
Complete the form below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 1–10

Complete the form below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

Kiwi Air Customer Complaint Form

Customer has been trying to make a booking using the internet

Booking details
• Name: Janet 1 …………..

• Ticket details: one adult and three children

• (Wellington to Christchurch) – four 2 …………… tickets

Flight details:
• November 10th – 15th

• Time: departing at 3 ………… a.m.

Details of the problem
• the website changes the rate for one child

• the website keep giving the customer 4 ………….. messages

Customer informed that Kiwi Air policy:
• allows no more than 5 ………… child fares per adult
(this applies to domestic flights only)

Customer’s reasons for complaint
• none of her children are 6 ……….. years old or older

• child passengers require less 7 ……………..

• the rule is unfair for large families and 8 ………….. groups

Customer invited to send written complaint
• letter to go to the 9 ……………

• reply predicted within a week

• reference number: 10 …………..

items.prompt_html: Booking details Name: Janet 1 …………..
(Wellington to Christchurch) – four 2 …………… tickets
Time: departing at 3 ………… a.m.
• the website keep giving the customer 4 ………….. messages

Customer informed that Kiwi Air policy:
• allows no more than 5 ………… child fares per adult

Customer’s reasons for complaint
• none of her children are 6 ……….. years old or older
• child passengers require less 7 ……………..
• the rule is unfair for large families and 8 ………….. groups

Customer invited to send written complaint
• letter to go to the 9 ……………
• reference number: 10 …………..

question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 11–14

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

Linwood Apartment Complex

11 When was the first apartment complex completed?

A 1 year ago

B 3 years ago

C 5 years ago

12 What does the manager say he enjoys most about the job?

A resolving problems between residents

B dealing with financial matters

C organizing building maintenance

13 Regarding restrictions in the complex, the manager says

A only some types of flowers can be planted

B only the interiors of apartments can be painted

C only certain colors can be used on the walls

14 What does the manager ask residents to do for the welcome party?

A bring food

B prepare name tags

C decorate the hall

options.label: A

options.label: C
options.text: 5 years ago

question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 21–24

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

The history and development of bicycle

21 Izzy believes the ‘safety bicycle’ is significant because it was

A specifically made for children to use.

B the first bicycle to be similar to modern bicycles.

C designed to travel at higher speeds than previous models.

22 What effect of bicycles on the 19th-century working classes does Max want to highlight?

A how people spent their free time

B where people were employed

C the way people found romantic partners

23 What will Max and Izzy stress about attitudes to bicycles in the 20th century?

A The popularity of cars reduced the status of bicycles in the 1950s.

B New designs led to an increase in sales of bicycles in the 1960s.

C An interest in fitness promoted demand for bicycles in the 1980s.

24 What does Max say about a scheme to provide free bikes to school pupils?

A He wants to see more evidence of its benefits.

B He thinks it could cost the government a lot of money.

C He questions whether all parents would support it.

items.prompt_html: 21 Izzy believes the ‘safety bicycle’ is significant because it was

options.label: A
options.text: specifically made for children to use.

options.label: B
options.text: the first bicycle to be similar to modern bicycles.

options.label: C
options.text: designed to travel at higher speeds than previous models.

items.prompt_html: 22 What effect of bicycles on the 19th-century working classes does Max want to highlight?

options.label: A
options.text: how people spent their free time

options.label: B
options.text: where people were employed

options.label: C
options.text: the way people found romantic partners

items.prompt_html: 23 What will Max and Izzy stress about attitudes to bicycles in the 20th century?

options.label: A
options.text: The popularity of cars reduced the status of bicycles in the 1950s.

options.label: B
options.text: New designs led to an increase in sales of bicycles in the 1960s.

options.label: C
options.text: An interest in fitness promoted demand for bicycles in the 1980s.

items.prompt_html: 24 What does Max say about a scheme to provide free bikes to school pupils?

options.label: A
options.text: He wants to see more evidence of its benefits.

options.label: B
options.text: He thinks it could cost the government a lot of money.

options.label: C
options.text: He questions whether all parents would support it.

question_groups.instruction:
Which reason did each of the following people give for using a bike?
Choose the correct answer from the box.
question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 25–30

Reasons
A Cycling is a convenient way to travel.
B Bikes are environmentally friendly.
C Riding a bike can be a social activity.
D Bikes are a cheap form of transport.
E Cycling is a way to spend time outdoors.
F Riding a bike is good for mental health.
G Riding a bike is good exercise.
H Cycling can be done by all ages.

items.prompt_html: 25 David
26 Marlene
27 Annabelle
28 Rashid
29 Saira
30 Giovanni

options.label: A
options.text: Cycling is a convenient way to travel.

options.label: B
options.text: Bikes are environmentally friendly.

options.label: C
options.text: Riding a bike can be a social activity.

options.label: D
options.text: Bikes are a cheap form of transport.

options.label: E
options.text: Cycling is a way to spend time outdoors.

options.label: F
options.text: Riding a bike is good for mental health.

options.label: G
options.text: Riding a bike is good exercise.

options.label: H
options.text: Cycling can be done by all ages.

question_groups.instruction:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 31–40

Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

The Patagon People of South America

Clothing
• One type of coat is culturally important.

• Young guanaco were used to make the coats because of their 31 ……… skin.

• Men hunted the guanaco.

• Women sewed the skins together into coats.

• Scrapers made from 32 ……….. are today displayed in museums.

• Colours from natural 33 …………. were used.

The arrival of the Spanish
• More guanaco were caught because of the use of the 34 ………… for hunting.

• Coats played a 35 ……… role rather than just being for personal use.

• New materials such as 36 ……….. improved the scraping process.

Conclusions
• Coat designs date from pre-historic times – similar designs are found inside 37 …………….

• Coat designs may have been a link with people's 38 …………

• Large Patagon settlements were not built on the 39 ………….

• Shepherds still make 40 ………….. using similar techniques.

items.prompt_html: • Young guanaco were used to make the coats because of their 31 ……… skin.
• Scrapers made from 32 ……….. are today displayed in museums.
• Colours from natural 33 …………. were used.

The arrival of the Spanish
• More guanaco were caught because of the use of the 34 ………… for hunting.
• Coats played a 35 ……… role rather than just being for personal use.
• New materials such as 36 ……….. improved the scraping process.

Conclusions
• Coat designs date from pre-historic times – similar designs are found inside 37 …………….
• Coat designs may have been a link with people's 38 …………
• Large Patagon settlements were not built on the 39 ………….
• Shepherds still make 40 ………….. using similar techniques.
